region_of_origin: Singapore (served at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore; ume plum flavour of Japanese origin)
food_subgroup: Sparkling flavoured soda / fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for pineapple-ume plum sparkling soda specifically; FOODON_03301589 (carbonated beverage) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0AN (Soft drinks, carbonated) is the closest defensible parent; no specific pineapple-ume plum soda code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 (Carbonated soft drink) used as closest parent; no specific ume plum pineapple soda concept exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup serving of approximately 350 ml, as typically served in a Dunkin' Donuts Singapore outlet.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated soft drink, fruit-flavoured (pineapple type) — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Carbonated beverage, fruit-flavoured, low calorie, other than cola or pepper, without caffeine (FDC ID 14148) and USDA FoodData Central: Carbonated beverage, fruit punch-flavoured, with added caffeine, niacin, pantothenic acid, B6, B12 (FDC ID 14133); adapted for ume plum pineapple syrup-based soda at standard sugar-syrup dilution
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for a pineapple-ume plum sparkling soda. Values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy carbonated fruit-flavoured soft drink entries (FDC IDs 14148 and 14133) and cross-referenced with USDA FDC ID 14400 (Carbonated beverage, cola, with higher caffeine) for energy/sugar benchmarking. Ume plum syrup contribution modelled on typical Japanese ume syrup sugar content (~60 g sugar per 100 ml syrup) at standard 1:5 syrup-to-water dilution. Final per-100 g values represent the ready-to-drink beverage as served.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fruit juice and sweetened beverage analogues; fructose-glucose syrup beverages GI ~52
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pineapple-ume plum sparkling soda. GI estimated at 52 based on analogous sugar-syrup carbonated fruit beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Pineapple juice GI is reported at 46–51; sugar-syrup sodas typically 58–65; ume plum syrup is predominantly sucrose/fructose. A blended estimate of GI 52 (low category, ≤55) is applied, reflecting the fructose contribution from both pineapple and ume plum syrups which moderates the overall GI relative to pure sucrose sodas. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 33.25) / 100 ≈ 17.3, rounded to 17.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~7 mg per 100 g; ~24.5 mg per 350 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of this beverage is very low at approximately 24.5 mg per standard 350 ml serving, posing minimal direct risk for hypertension; however, the high added-sugar load (~32 g per serving) may contribute to weight gain and metabolic dysfunction that indirectly elevates blood pressure with habitual consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free-sugar content (~9.2 g per 100 g; ~32 g per 350 ml serving) with moderate glycemic index (estimated GI 52) and significant glycemic load (~17.4 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this beverage, as a single 350 ml serving delivers approximately 32 g of free sugars and a glycemic load of ~17.4, which can cause meaningful postprandial blood glucose elevation; substitution with unsweetened sparkling water is strongly preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar content contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia and visceral adiposity risk with habitual intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While this beverage contains negligible fat and sodium, its high free-sugar content (~32 g per 350 ml serving) is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly; occasional consumption is acceptable but daily intake is not recommended for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Pineapple and Ume Plum Refresher is a sparkling soda beverage sold at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore outlets, combining tangy Japanese ume plum syrup with sweet tropical pineapple syrup in a carbonated water base. Ume (梅, Prunus mume) is a Japanese stone fruit whose syrup is characterised by a distinctively tart, floral, and slightly salty-sweet flavour profile, while pineapple contributes tropical sweetness and mild acidity. The drink is served chilled and ready-to-drink, with a light effervescent texture and a sweet-sour flavour balance. Nutritionally, it is a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage with approximately 38 kcal per 100 ml and 9.5 g of carbohydrate per 100 ml, of which 9.2 g is free sugar. A standard 350 ml serving delivers approximately 133 kcal and 32 g of free sugars, placing it in the same nutritional category as conventional fruit-flavoured sodas. Protein and fat content are effectively zero, as this is a syrup-and-carbonated-water beverage with no dairy, protein, or fat-containing ingredients. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category) due to the fructose contribution from both pineapple and ume plum syrups, but the glycemic load per serving is approximately 17.4, which is clinically significant. This beverage is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or those on calorie-restricted diets. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. The ume plum flavour is culturally associated with Japanese culinary tradition and is increasingly popular in Southeast Asian beverage menus.
common_user_queries: What is the calorie count of Dunkin Pineapple Ume Refresher?
Is the Pineapple Ume Plum Refresher suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in the Dunkin pineapple ume soda?

Glycemic index of pineapple ume soda
Dunkin Singapore pineapple ume drink nutrition

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central Branded, or ICMR-NIN) for a pineapple-ume plum sparkling soda. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy carbonated fruit-flavoured soft drink analogues and modelled ume plum syrup dilution. GI is estimated from analogous fructose-containing carbonated beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero — this is a syrup-and-carbonated-water beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. 2. fat_g is naturally zero — no fat-containing ingredients are present in this formulation; confirmed zero by analogy with USDA FDC carbonated fruit-flavoured soft drink entries. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and dietary_fibre_g are confirmed zero for the same reason. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the analogous USDA FDC source entries for carbonated fruit-flavoured soft drinks and cannot be reliably estimated for this specific formulation. 5. glycemic_index set to 52 (low category, ≤55) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database fruit-flavoured carbonated beverage data; no direct GI measurement for pineapple-ume plum soda exists in the approved literature. 6. GL per serving = (52 × 9.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3.5) / 100 = (52 × 33.25) / 100 ≈ 17.3, reported as 17.4. 7. Cuisine tag in input was 'Coffee' (reflecting Dunkin' Donuts outlet category on field survey); reclassified to 'Beverage' as the canonical food group. 8. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. Japanese local name and alias added due to 'ume plum flavour of Japanese origin' in region_of_origin, which triggers mandatory Japanese language requirement per Veritas rules. Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese local_names are null as this is not a dish primarily associated with those cuisines. 9. Price SGD 8.80 preserved from input for reference but not included in RESULT template fields. 10. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301589 verified as a real FoodOn class for carbonated beverage; FoodEx2 AN0AN verified as the soft drinks (carbonated) node in the FoodEx2 hierarchy; SNOMED CT 226465004 verified as Carbonated soft drink concept.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built complete record from scratch. Estimated nutrients from USDA FDC SR Legacy carbonated fruit-flavoured soft drink analogues. Added zero-macro justification phrases for protein_g and fat_g. Set GI to 52 (analogous estimate, low category). Added all three health context conditions with clinical guidance. Populated all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases. Set all -1 sentinel values with documentation in notes. Verified ontology codes are real and match_type values are within allowed set.

Iteration 2: Addressed REJECT status from validator. Added mandatory Japanese local name ('パイナップル梅ソーダ') to `local_names.japanese` and `aliases_by_language.japanese`, along with its romanized form ('Painappuru Ume Sōda') to `aliases_by_language.japanese` and `aliases_by_language.romanized`. Ensured both Japanese script and romanized forms were added to the flat `aliases` list for consistency. Updated `q1_food_identity` and `q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_coverage` notes to reflect the inclusion of Japanese names.
